name: Three-way quicksort for arrays with duplicates

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). For each case, line A is N >= 1 and line B is N space-separated integers. For each case, output the sorted integers as space-separated values on their own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself (split into <pivot, ==pivot, >pivot regions, must handle duplicates efficiently). Do NOT call sorted() or list.sort() on the input data.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; L=$(mktemp); printf %s  | base64 -d > "$L"; FPAT='sorted\s*\(|\.sort\s*\(' python3 "$L" qs.py py || { echo 'forbidden api'; rm -f "$L"; exit 1; }; rm -f "$L"; R=$(mktemp -d); cp input.txt "$R/in.txt"; ( cd "$R" && python3 /tmp/eval_qs/qs.py < in.txt ) > out.txt; rm -rf "$R"; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T, then per case line A is N and line B is N space-separated integers. Output each sorted array on its own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself — do NOT use sort.Ints, sort.Slice, sort.Sort, or any std sort. Compile with `go build -o qs qs.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; L=$(mktemp); printf %s  | base64 -d > "$L"; FPAT='sort\.(Ints|Slice|Sort|Strings|SliceStable|IntSlice)\b' python3 "$L" qs.go c || { echo 'forbidden api'; rm -f "$L"; exit 1; }; rm -f "$L"; go build -o /tmp/eval_qs/qs /tmp/eval_qs/qs.go; R=$(mktemp -d); cp input.txt "$R/in.txt"; ( cd "$R" && /tmp/eval_qs/qs < in.txt ) > out.txt; rm -rf "$R"; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.c — reads from stdin: line 1 is T, then per case line A is N and line B is N space-separated integers. Output each sorted array on its own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself — do NOT call qsort. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o qs qs.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; L=$(mktemp); printf %s  | base64 -d > "$L"; FPAT='\bqsort\s*\(' python3 "$L" qs.c c || { echo 'forbidden api'; rm -f "$L"; exit 1; }; rm -f "$L"; gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o /tmp/eval_qs/qs /tmp/eval_qs/qs.c; R=$(mktemp -d); cp input.txt "$R/in.txt"; ( cd "$R" && /tmp/eval_qs/qs < in.txt ) > out.txt; rm -rf "$R"; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.cpp — reads from stdin: line 1 is T, then per case line A is N and line B is N space-separated integers. Output each sorted array on its own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself — do NOT call std::sort, std::stable_sort, std::partial_sort, std::nth_element, or qsort. Must compile cleanly with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o qs qs.cpp`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; L=$(mktemp); printf %s  | base64 -d > "$L"; FPAT='std::(sort|stable_sort|partial_sort|nth_element)\b|\bqsort\s*\(' python3 "$L" qs.cpp c || { echo 'forbidden api'; rm -f "$L"; exit 1; }; rm -f "$L"; g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o /tmp/eval_qs/qs /tmp/eval_qs/qs.cpp; R=$(mktemp -d); cp input.txt "$R/in.txt"; ( cd "$R" && /tmp/eval_qs/qs < in.txt ) > out.txt; rm -rf "$R"; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.rs — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (the test case count). For each case, line A is N (N >= 1) and line B is N space-separated integers. For each case, output the sorted integers (ascending) as space-separated values on their own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself — partition into three regions (< pivot, == pivot, > pivot) so runs of equal keys are handled efficiently. Do NOT call any standard-library sort (no slice::sort, sort_unstable, sort_by, BinaryHeap-based sorting, etc.). Idiomatic Rust 2021+; compile with `rustc -O qs.rs -o qs`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; L=$(mktemp); printf %s  | base64 -d > "$L"; FPAT='\.sort(_unstable|_by|_by_key)?\s*\(|BinaryHeap' python3 "$L" qs.rs c || { echo 'forbidden api'; rm -f "$L"; exit 1; }; rm -f "$L"; rustc -O /tmp/eval_qs/qs.rs -o /tmp/eval_qs/qs 2>/dev/null; R=$(mktemp -d); cp input.txt "$R/in.txt"; ( cd "$R" && /tmp/eval_qs/qs < in.txt ) > out.txt; rm -rf "$R"; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (the test case count). For each case, line A is N (N >= 1) and line B is N space-separated integers. For each case, output the sorted integers (ascending) as space-separated values on their own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself — partition into three regions (< pivot, == pivot, > pivot) so runs of equal keys are handled efficiently. Do NOT call any standard-library sort (no std.sort.*, std.mem.sort). Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast qs.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; L=$(mktemp); printf %s  | base64 -d > "$L"; FPAT='std\.sort\.|std\.mem\.sort' python3 "$L" qs.zig c || { echo 'forbidden api'; rm -f "$L"; exit 1; }; rm -f "$L"; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast /tmp/eval_qs/qs.zig -femit-bin=/tmp/eval_qs/qs; R=$(mktemp -d); cp input.txt "$R/in.txt"; ( cd "$R" && /tmp/eval_qs/qs < in.txt ) > out.txt; rm -rf "$R"; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
